perturbation_predictions.expected_behavior: Removing or relabeling the filter signature must break hard KcsA grammar.
perturbation_predictions.falsifiable_if: generic channel annotation alone preserves hard KcsA grammar

perturbation_predictions.expected_behavior: Removing K+ selectivity evidence must break or invalidate the hard selectivity grammar.
perturbation_predictions.falsifiable_if: hard KcsA grammar remains validated without ion-selectivity evidence

perturbation_predictions.expected_behavior: Generic soluble/channel annotation alone must not preserve membrane-pore grammar.
perturbation_predictions.falsifiable_if: soluble-only annotation preserves hard KcsA grammar

perturbation_predictions.expected_behavior: Oligomer/interface context may weaken mechanism support but must not become a whole-fold prediction.
perturbation_predictions.falsifiable_if: oligomer context is converted into a whole-fold claim

scientist_question: Can the grammar identify that perturbations affecting filter/ion-selectivity evidence should matter more than generic channel annotation?
